original_description: Audit Claude Code's approved Bash commands (permissions.allow) for security risks. Scans settings.json for dangerous patterns like Bash(*), sudo, rm -rf, curl|sh. Triggers: "audit permissions", "cc-safe", "permission audit", reviewing permissions.allow, or after update-config modifies Bash permissions. ONLY for Claude Code's own permissions.allow — not npm/pip audit, chmod, IAM, or general security review.
best_description: Audit Claude Code's approved Bash commands (permissions.allow) for security risks. Scans settings.json for dangerous patterns like Bash(*), sudo, rm -rf, curl|sh. Triggers: "audit permissions", "cc-safe", "permission audit", reviewing permissions.allow, or after update-config modifies Bash permissions. ONLY for Claude Code's own permissions.allow — not npm/pip audit, chmod, IAM, or general security review.
final_description:
Use when the user wants to review, audit, or assess the security of commands that Claude Code is allowed to run automatically — specifically entries in `permissions.allow` inside `settings.json`. Covers questions like whether dangerous patterns (wildcards, sudo, rm -rf, curl|sh) are present in the allow list, whether specific entries pose security risks, or whether the overall set of auto-approved commands is safe. Also use when someone flags a risky entry that was added to Claude Code's approved commands.

Do NOT use for: auditing bash scripts in a project's codebase, deploy pipelines, or CI scripts — this skill only covers Claude Code's own runtime permissions in settings.json.

history.description: Audit Claude Code's approved Bash commands (permissions.allow) for security risks. Scans settings.json for dangerous patterns like Bash(*), sudo, rm -rf, curl|sh. Triggers: "audit permissions", "cc-safe", "permission audit", reviewing permissions.allow, or after update-config modifies Bash permissions. ONLY for Claude Code's own permissions.allow — not npm/pip audit, chmod, IAM, or general security review.

train_results.query: permission audit for my claude code setup
our security team wants a review of what shell commands claude code can run autonomously
we added sudo and rm -rf to approved commands last week, someone flagged it in code review, can you review the whole allow list
audit my claude code permissions
is Bash(*) in my settings.json a security risk
can you check settings.json for dangerous bash commands
check if our GraphQL endpoints have any permission issues
review what permissions the app requests from the OS at runtime
audit our REST API for security vulnerabilities before we ship
audit the bash scripts in our deploy pipeline for risky commands
check if any npm packages in package.json have known CVEs
our CI pipeline runs curl | sh to install deps, is that safe

test_results.query: check my claude settings for dangerous shell permissions
I just updated my permissions.allow list, can you check if any of the new entries are risky
just ran update-config to add some bash permissions, audit them pls
I want to stress test the auth endpoints and find vulnerabilities in our API
audit the RBAC configuration in our database schema
scan my codebase for hardcoded credentials or insecure patterns
review my app's user permission model for privilege escalation risks
